# Tortoise ORM. Add additional field to PydanticListModel class generated by pydantic_queryset_creator function
[Link to question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76799760/tortoise-orm-add-additional-field-to-pydanticlistmodel-class-generated-by-pydan)
**Creation Date:** 1690754248
**Score:** 1
**Tags:** fastapi, pydantic, tortoise-orm
## Question Body
<p>Can you tell me how, when using <code>tortoise.contrib.pydantic.pydantic_queryset_creator</code>, to add an additional field that is generated when querying the database?</p>
<p>Here is the query:</p>
<pre><code>categories = await EquipmentCategory \
    .filter(equipment__organization__inn=organization_inn) \
    .annotate(equipment_count=functions.Count('equipment')) \
    .order_by('-equipment_count') \
    .limit(3) \
    .all()
</code></pre>
<p>The <code>pydantic_queryset_creator(EquipmentCategory)</code> class does not contain the <code>equipment_count</code> field, and it is needed during serialization.</p>
<p>I tried to inherit and redefine the field:</p>
<pre><code>class EquipmentCategoryListPydantic(pydantic_queryset_creator(EquipmentCategory)):
    equipment_count: int
</code></pre>
<p>But it leads to the exception:</p>
<pre><code>ValueError: __root__ cannot be mixed with other fields

</code></pre>
<p>I really don't want to rewrite everything from scratch (using <code>pydantic.BaseModel</code>) and repeat the whole code twice!</p>
<p>I hope for your help!</p>

## Answers
### Answer ID: 76801263
<p>Solved! (But is it a good solution?).</p>
<p>I inherited the abstract ORM class <code>EquipmentCategoryWithEquipmentCount</code> from the original ORM class <code>EquipmentCategory</code> and added the <code>equipment_count</code> field to it. Later on in <code>pydantic_queryset_creator</code> I just used this class:</p>
<pre><code>class EquipmentCategory(models.Model):
    name = fields.CharField(max_length=255)
    created_at = fields.DatetimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    class PydanticMeta:
        exclude = [&quot;created_at&quot;, &quot;equipment&quot;]


class EquipmentCategoryWithEquipmentCount(EquipmentCategory):
    equipment_count = fields.IntField()

    class Meta:
        abstract = True

EquipmentCategoryListSchema = pydantic_queryset_creator(EquipmentCategoryWithEquipmentCount)
</code></pre>
